content:
# John Chapter 8 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_008_page_001.jpg, JHN_008_page_002.jpg

[8:1-11 — The Pericope Adulterae is not present in the Sinaiticus manuscript. The text continues directly from 7:52.]

12. Again therefore the Yeshua spoke to them, saying, "I am the light of the world. The [one] following me will not walk in the darkness, but will have the light of the life."

13. Then the Pharisaioi said to him, "You testify concerning yourself; the testimony of you is not true."

14. The Yeshua answered and said to them, "Even if I testify concerning myself, the testimony of me is true, because I know from where I came and where I go; but you do not know from where I come or where I go.

15. You judge according to the flesh; I judge no one.

16. And even if I judge, the judgment of me is true, because I am not alone, but I and the [one] having sent me.

17. And in the law of you it is written that the testimony of two men is true.

18. I am the [one] testifying concerning myself, and the Father having sent me testifies concerning me."

19. Then they said to him, "Where is the father of you?" The Yeshua answered, "You know neither me nor the Father of me. If you had known me, you would have known the Father of me also."

20. These words he spoke in the treasury, teaching in the temple; and no one seized him, because the hour of him had not yet come.

21. Then he said to them again, "I go away, and you will seek me, and in the sin of you you will die. Where I go, you are not able to come."

22. Then the Ioudaioi said, "Will he kill himself, because he says, 'Where I go, you are not able to come'?"

23. And he said to them, "You are from the [things] below; I am from the [things] above. You are from this world; I am not from this world.

24. Therefore I said to you that you will die in the sins of you; for if you do not believe that I am, you will die in the sins of you."

25. Then they said to him, "You, who are you?" The Yeshua said to them, "What I have been telling you from the beginning.

26. I have many things to speak and to judge concerning you; but the [one] having sent me is true, and I, what I heard from him, these things I speak to the world."

27. They did not know that he was speaking to them [of] the Father.

28. Then the Yeshua said, "When you lift up the Son of the Man, then you will know that I am, and from myself I do nothing, but just as the Father taught me, these things I speak.

29. And the [one] having sent me is with me; he has not left me alone, because I always do the things pleasing to him."

30. As he was saying these things, many believed in him.

31. Then the Yeshua said to the Ioudaioi who had believed him, "If you remain in the word of me, you are truly disciples of me,

32. and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free."

33. They answered him, "Seed of Abraam we are, and to no one have we been enslaved ever. How do you say, 'You will become free'?"

34. The Yeshua answered them, "Amen, amen, I say to you that everyone doing the sin is a slave of the sin.

35. And the slave does not remain in the house to the age; the son remains to the age.

36. If therefore the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.

37. I know that you are seed of Abraam; but you seek to kill me, because the word of me has no room in you.

38. I speak what I have seen with the Father; and you therefore do what you heard from the father."

39. They answered and said to him, "The father of us is Abraam." The Yeshua says to them, "If you are children of Abraam, do the works of Abraam.

40. But now you seek to kill me, a man who has spoken to you the truth that I heard from the God. This Abraam did not do.

41. You do the works of the father of you." They said to him, "We were not born from fornication; we have one Father — the God."

42. The Yeshua said to them, "If the God were the father of you, you would love me, for I came forth from the God and have come; for I have not come from myself, but that [one] sent me.

43. Why do you not understand the speech of me? Because you are not able to hear the word of me.

44. You are from the father, the devil, and the desires of the father of you you wish to do. That [one] was a murderer from [the] beginning, and in the truth he has not stood, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks the lie, from the own he speaks, because he is a liar and the father of it.

45. But because I speak the truth, you do not believe me.

46. Who of you convicts me of sin? If I speak truth, why do you not believe me?

47. The [one] being from the God hears the words of the God; because of this you do not hear, because from the God you are not."

48. The Ioudaioi answered and said to him, "Do we not rightly say that you are a Samaritanos and have a demon?"

49. The Yeshua answered, "I do not have a demon, but I honor the Father of me, and you dishonor me.

50. But I do not seek the glory of me; there is [one] seeking and judging.

51. Amen, amen, I say to you, if anyone keeps the word of me, he will not see death to the age."

52. The Ioudaioi said to him, "Now we know that you have a demon. Abraam died and the prophets, and you say, 'If anyone keeps the word of me, he will not taste death to the age.'

53. Are you greater than the father of us Abraam, who died? And the prophets died. Whom do you make yourself?"

54. The Yeshua answered, "If I glorify myself, the glory of me is nothing; it is the Father of me who glorifies me, whom you say that he is the God of you.

55. And you have not known him; but I know him. And if I say that I do not know him, I will be like you, a liar. But I know him and the word of him I keep.

56. Abraam the father of you rejoiced that he might see the day of me, and he saw and was glad."

57. Then the Ioudaioi said to him, "You are not yet fifty years [old], and Abraam has seen you?"

58. The Yeshua said to them, "Amen, amen, I say to you, before Abraam was born, I am."

59. Then they picked up stones that they might throw [them] at him; but the Yeshua was hidden and went out of the temple.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.12 — "I am the light of the world":** The second great "I AM" statement with predicate. Jesus speaks during or after the Tabernacles light ceremony, when four giant candelabra were lit in the Court of Women, illuminating the entire temple. Jesus replaces the ritual light with himself.

2. **v.24, 28, 58 — ἐγώ εἰμι ("I am"):** Three absolute uses of "I am" without a predicate — the most explicit divine name claims in the Gospel. In v.24 and v.28, "I am" stands alone as a condition and a promise. In v.58, "before Abraham was born, I am" — the present tense "I am" contrasted with the aorist "was born" (γενέσθαι) claims pre-existence and divine identity.

3. **v.38 — "the father":** Sinaiticus appears to read "your father" (τοῦ πατρός ὑμῶν) in the second clause, making the contrast explicit: Jesus does what he has seen with *his* Father; they do what they heard from *their* father (i.e., the devil, as v.44 makes explicit).

4. **v.57 — "Abraam has seen you?":** Sinaiticus reads ἑώρακέν σε ("has seen you") — "Has Abraham seen you?" — rather than the variant "Have you seen Abraham?" This reading is more dramatic: the Jews are incredulous not that Jesus saw Abraham, but that Abraham could have seen Jesus.

5. **v.59 — "was hidden":** ἐκρύβη — passive or middle. Jesus does not flee; he is "hidden" — possibly a divine passive, indicating God's protection. The attempt to stone him fails because his hour has not come.

— EMET, AI Translator
